title: Support per-crew default agent in rig settings: allow crew_agents.bob=codex in settings/config.json so specific named crew get a default agent without --agent flag every time. Resolution: explicit --agent > crew_agents.<name> > rig agent > town default.

Expected: refinery detects branch commit already in master, auto-closes corresponding MR beads, and advances queue without manual bead closure.

Example errors: sl-wisp-82j had sl-wisp-dixxf + sl-wisp-hhpkm attached; sss-wisp-ati6 had sss-wisp-c58o attached. Command failed after spawning a new polecat, leaving extra sessions. Recovery required gt sling --force which burns stale molecules. Expected: non-destructive stale-molecule recovery path or automatic rebind to existing active molecule.
title: Sling cannot recover stale attached molecules without --force burn

description: Multiple workers hit 'GraphQL: API rate limit already exceeded for user ID 12613144' on commands like gh label list / gh issue list, while gh api rate_limit showed core REST quota remaining. Workers had to manually switch to REST endpoints (gh api repos/<repo>/labels, etc.) to continue. Expected: workflow templates and worker helpers automatically fallback to REST when GraphQL quota is exhausted.
title: Intake/validity workflows rely on GraphQL-only gh commands and stall under GraphQL limit

description: After fresh HQ beads DB initialization, 'gt compact report' fails with: 'all sends failed: deacon/: invalid recipient "deacon/": no agent found'. The HQ beads db had issue_prefix missing (fixed manually via 'bd config set issue_prefix hq'), but the agent bead for deacon/ was never seeded. The compact report tries to send mail to 'deacon/' which doesn't exist as an agent bead in the fresh HQ database. Needs: either (1) seed agent beads during HQ init, or (2) make gt compact report tolerate missing agent beads gracefully.
title: gt compact report fails: deacon/ agent not found in fresh HQ db
mol-orphan-scan formula broken: vars.scope has both required:true and default, causing validation failure on gt sling

close_reason: Fixed by gt doctor --fix which created 2 missing agent beads. Root cause: agent beads weren't registered in mail system. Now resolved.
description: gt mail send deacon/ and gt mail send mayor/ both fail with 'Error: all sends failed: <identity>/: invalid recipient: no agent found'. This breaks: 1) gt compact report (tries to send to deacon/), 2) Deacon lifecycle messaging, 3) cross-agent notifications. The agent beads for deacon and mayor are not registered in the mail system. Need to investigate how agent beads get created and why they're missing for hq-deacon and hq-mayor.
title: gt mail: deacon/ and mayor/ fail with 'no agent found'
mol-orphan-scan formula broken: vars.scope has both required:true and default, causing 'formula validation failed' error when slung to dogs

description: EnsureGitignorePatterns() in overlay.go adds .runtime/, .claude/commands/, and .logs/ but misses state.json (crew worker metadata) and .beads/ (redirect stub). Both are GT infrastructure that show up as untracked in the project repo. Every new crew workspace starts with a dirty git status. Fix: Add state.json and .beads/ to the required patterns in EnsureGitignorePatterns().
title: gt crew add doesn't gitignore state.json or .beads/ in crew workspaces

description: When slinging to a rig (e.g. auditor), gt sling looks for mol-polecat-work in auditor/mayor/rig/ and fails if not found. It should fall back to the central formula store (gastown/.beads/formulas/). Formulas are shared infrastructure, not per-rig artifacts. Currently requires --hook-raw-bead workaround which skips lifecycle instructions entirely.
title: gt sling formula resolution should fall back to central GT formulas, not only look in target rig

close_reason: Fixed: handoff now uses role_agents resolution
description:
dispatched_by: mayor

## Bug

In `internal/cmd/handoff.go`, `buildRestartCommand()` at line 541 calls:

```go
runtimeCmd = config.GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt(rigPath, beacon)
```

This uses `ResolveAgentConfig()` which resolves the **default** agent, ignoring the `role_agents` map in town/rig settings. So when a witness (configured as `claude-haiku` via `role_agents.witness`) does a self-handoff, it restarts as the default agent (opus) instead of haiku.

## Expected

Handoff should preserve the role-based agent config. The witness manager's `buildWitnessStartCommand()` correctly uses `BuildAgentStartupCommandWithAgentOverride()` which calls `ResolveRoleAgentConfig()` — the handoff path should do the same.

## Fix

In `buildRestartCommand()`, replace the call to `GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt()` with role-aware resolution, e.g.:

```go
simpleRole := config.ExtractSimpleRole(gtRole)
runtimeCmd, err = config.BuildAgentStartupCommand(simpleRole, identity.Rig, townRoot, rigPath, beacon)
```

This ensures handoff respects `role_agents` from `settings/config.json`.

## Key files
- `internal/cmd/handoff.go` line ~541 (buildRestartCommand)
- `internal/config/loader.go` (ResolveRoleAgentConfig vs ResolveAgentConfig)
- `internal/witness/manager.go` line ~234 (correct pattern to follow)
- `~/gt/settings/config.json` (role_agents config)

Create a PR with the fix.
title: handoff.go buildRestartCommand uses GetRuntimeCommandWithPrompt which ignores role_agents config, causing self-handoff to lose model flag

description: gt mail send fails with 'prefix mismatch: database uses hq- (allowed: hq,hq-cv) but ID msg-xxx doesnt match any allowed prefix'. Started happening mid-session — earlier sends in the same session worked fine. Binary is 7 commits behind (built from 3336462, repo at f32c829b2630). Might be related to stale binary or database state change.
title: gt mail send broken: prefix mismatch on msg- IDs
